programs.title: Responsible AI Program
programs.summary: Four-week Responsible AI learning arc for foundations, ethics, societal impact, human agency, and capstone practice.

modules.title: Foundations
modules.objective: Explain what AI systems are, where they show up, and how to evaluate their limits.
facilitator_notes: Start with the visible system boundary before discussing model capability.
Ask learners to name what evidence they would need before trusting the output.
discussion_prompts: Where does the human decision enter this workflow?
What would make this visual misleading for a beginner?

critique_criteria.label: Foundation clarity
critique_criteria.prompt: Does the image explain the AI concept without hiding uncertainty?

critique_criteria.label: Human agency
critique_criteria.prompt: Does the image show where people direct, evaluate, or override the system?

modules.title: Ethics And Impact
modules.objective: Connect generated examples to bias, privacy, transparency, and community impact decisions.
facilitator_notes: Keep the conversation grounded in who benefits, who is exposed, and who can contest the output.
Use image candidates as critique objects, not as proof that the concept is solved.
discussion_prompts: What harm could this image normalize if published without context?
Who should review this before it becomes a teaching asset?

critique_criteria.label: Ethical safety
critique_criteria.prompt: Does the image surface risk, bias, and affected communities without sensationalizing them?
critique_criteria.scale: risky / usable with context / safe to teach

critique_criteria.label: Community context
critique_criteria.prompt: Does the image avoid generic AI symbolism when a community-specific frame is needed?

modules.title: Capstone Practice
modules.objective: Use a visual artifact to defend a responsible AI decision and identify the next human checkpoint.
facilitator_notes: Have learners annotate the asset with the decision it supports and the decision it cannot make.
Require one next action: publish, revise, block, or gather missing evidence.
discussion_prompts: What would you change before using this with a real audience?
Which criterion matters most for this asset: clarity, safety, accessibility, or brand fit?

critique_criteria.label: Decision readiness
critique_criteria.prompt: Does the asset support a clear responsible AI decision and next checkpoint?

critique_criteria.label: Teaching transfer
critique_criteria.prompt: Can a learner apply the idea to a new scenario after seeing this asset?

programs.title: Upgrade AI
programs.summary: Marketing, newsletter, podcast, and training visuals that turn AI practice into teachable public formats.

modules.title: Practical Prompting
modules.objective: Translate a work goal into useful prompt inputs, constraints, and review criteria.
facilitator_notes: Ask learners to separate intent, constraints, examples, and review criteria before generating.
Treat the first output as a diagnostic for prompt quality.
discussion_prompts: Which missing constraint would most improve this generation?
What would you tell the model to preserve in a rerun?

critique_criteria.label: Prompt fit
critique_criteria.prompt: Does the output reflect the stated intent and constraints?

modules.title: Publishing System
modules.objective: Turn generated assets into a repeatable newsletter, podcast, or campaign package.
facilitator_notes: Review the asset as part of a package, not as a standalone image.
Check whether the image implies a promise the content does not deliver.
discussion_prompts: Where should this asset appear in the campaign sequence?
What metadata would make it easier to reuse later?

critique_criteria.label: Package readiness
critique_criteria.prompt: Can the asset move cleanly into a newsletter, podcast, or campaign bundle?

programs.title: HOPECODE
programs.summary: Knowledge gardens, power maps, relationship maps, values, skill trees, and anti-corporate systems thinking.

modules.title: Knowledge Garden
modules.objective: Map concepts, people, values, and artifacts as a living learning system.
facilitator_notes: Invite learners to add missing relationships rather than only critique the image composition.
Use the visual as a shared map for surfacing hidden assumptions.
discussion_prompts: Which concept is central, and which concept is only nearby?
What relationship is missing from this map?

critique_criteria.label: Map legibility
critique_criteria.prompt: Can a learner follow the relationships without a facilitator narrating every node?

modules.title: Power And Relationship Maps
modules.objective: Use visual mapping to reason about influence, trust, dependency, and care.
facilitator_notes: Ask where power is formal, informal, inherited, or contested.
Do not let the map flatten care relationships into generic network lines.
discussion_prompts: Who is missing from the map?
Which relationship would change the next action?

critique_criteria.label: Relationship accuracy
critique_criteria.prompt: Does the image distinguish influence, dependency, trust, and care?

programs.title: BC AI Ecosystem
programs.summary: Community, subgroup, event, and ecosystem diagrams for explaining the regional AI field.

modules.title: Community Map
modules.objective: Identify communities, subgroups, and stakeholder relationships in the BC AI ecosystem.
facilitator_notes: Review whether the visual helps someone orient inside the ecosystem quickly.
Flag any subgroup or community representation that needs owner review.
discussion_prompts: Which stakeholder relationship is easiest to understand?
Which relationship needs more context before publication?

critique_criteria.label: Ecosystem orientation
critique_criteria.prompt: Does the image make the community structure easier to navigate?

modules.title: Event Story
modules.objective: Turn event visuals into a coherent learning or public recap sequence.
facilitator_notes: Use event assets to reconstruct what changed for participants, not only what happened.
Check whether any public recap claim needs a human approval gate.
discussion_prompts: What is the learning arc across this event sequence?
Which image should become the public recap anchor?

critique_criteria.label: Recap fit
critique_criteria.prompt: Does the asset support a truthful and useful event story?
